### Summary of Main Findings in AI Applications in Breast Cancer Histopathology

#### Introduction
The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into breast cancer histopathology has significantly advanced the field, enabling more accurate and efficient analysis of tissue samples. This review synthesizes recent advancements in AI-driven models applied to breast cancer histopathology, highlighting their potential roles in supporting diagnostic and prognostic assessments.

#### Detection of Lymph Node Metastases
Several studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of deep learning (DL) algorithms in detecting lymph node metastases using hematoxylin and eosin (H&E)-stained images. These models achieve high accuracy in identifying microcalcifications, necrosis, and other morphological features indicative of metastatic spread. For instance, a study published in *Cancer Medicine* utilized a convolutional neural network (CNN) to detect lymph node metastases with an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.96, surpassing human pathologists' performance. The DL models learn complex patterns within the H&E images, capturing subtle changes that are often missed by human observers.

#### Assessment of Residual Tumor After Neoadjuvant Therapy
In the context of neoadjuvant therapy, AI models have shown promise in evaluating residual tumor size and extent post-treatment.
